title: A local LLM prototype filters web pages by meaning
friendlyTitle: A browser idea that hides content by what it means
summary: A developer describes a Firefox extension prototype that uses compact local language models to hide page content matching natural-language rules. Tests on an AMD GPU were fast enough for near-real-time browsing, while CPU-only use was slower and smaller models caused too many false positives.
friendlySummary: A developer made a Firefox test tool that uses a small AI model on the computer to hide web content you do not want to see. It uses simple instructions instead of only blocked words.
whyItMatters: Semantic filtering points toward a more personal kind of browser control: not just blocking ads or keywords, but reducing unwanted topics without sending browsing content to a remote model.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could one day help people control their feeds in a more personal way while keeping their browsing data on their own device.
